title: Track uses of in-source issue suppressions
description: Track uses of in-source issue suppressionsAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode SmellWhy is this an issue?This rule allows you to track the usage of theSuppressMessageattributes and#pragma warning disablemechanism.Noncompliant code example[SuppressMessage("", "S100")] ... #pragma warning disable S100 ... #pragma warning restore S100Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CIAvailable Since9.1Analyze code in youron-premise CIDeveloper EditionAvailable Since9.1©2025SonarSource Sàrl.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant:
[SuppressMessage("", "S100")] // Noncompliant - suppression should be tracked
public void myMethod() { }

checks.message: In-source issue suppression found. Review if the suppression is necessary or if the underlying issue should be fixed.

never_trigger_on: Code that properly addresses issues without suppression
logic.reasoning: This rule tracks the usage of SuppressMessage attributes and #pragma warning disable mechanisms. While sometimes necessary, suppressing warnings can hide real issues. Each suppression should be reviewed to determine if the underlying issue should be fixed instead.
